linting.notes: Linter configuration excludes test files and third_party directories. Pre-commit hooks also run golangci-lint.

testing.framework: Go testing with Ginkgo/Gomega BDD framework

test_tags.unit: Default: no build tags (excludes integration tests)

testing.mock_patterns: Uses mockoidc for OIDC provider mocking, miniredis for Redis mocking
testing.notes: 218 unit tests found. Integration tests require -tags integration flag. Tests use Ginkgo BDD framework with suite files (*_suite_test.go). Race detector is always enabled in CI.

ci.system: GitHub Actions (primary), Tekton (secondary for OpenShift CI)

gating_checks.name: Verify Code Generation
gating_checks.notes: Ensures generated docs match code structs

gating_checks.name: Test
gating_checks.notes: Runs unit tests with coverage reporting

gating_checks.name: Docker Build
gating_checks.notes: Builds multi-arch Docker image (amd64 only for PRs)

gating_checks.name: FIPS Compliance Check
gating_checks.notes: Verifies FIPS compliance for Red Hat builds (PRs to main)

ci.notes: CI runs on both push and pull_request events for all branches. FIPS compliance check only runs on PRs to main branch. Release builds test all architectures (amd64, arm64, ppc64le, s390x).

build_targets.fips: CGO_ENABLED=1 GOEXPERIMENT=strictfipsruntime go build -a -tags strictfipsruntime -ldflags="-X github.com/opendatahub-io/kube-auth-proxy/v1/pkg/version.VERSION=${VERSION}" -o kube-auth-proxy github.com/opendatahub-io/kube-auth-proxy/v1

build.notes: Project includes kube-rbac-proxy as a submodule/subproject with separate build. Uses go.work for multi-module workspace. FIPS builds require CGO and use UBI images.

conventions.test_naming: Go standard: func TestName(t *testing.T) for table tests, Ginkgo Describe/It/Context for BDD-style tests
conventions.suite_files: *_suite_test.go files set up Ginkgo test suites per package
conventions.import_style: Standard Go imports with blank line separating stdlib, external, and internal imports
conventions.build_tags: //go:build !integration for unit tests, //go:build integration for integration tests
conventions.code_organization: pkg/ for library code, cmd/ for binaries, providers/ for auth providers, test/integration/ for integration tests
conventions.notes: Uses Ginkgo/Gomega BDD framework extensively. Build tags separate unit and integration tests.

lint_commands.notes: Auto-fix command available but not validated

test_commands.notes: Equivalent to golangci-lint run && go test -v -race ./...

container_recipe.notes: Tests run quickly (~30s for full suite). No external dependencies required for unit tests. Integration tests tagged with 'integration' build tag.

results.output_snippet:

--- PASS: TestK8sTokenAuthentication_ValidToken (0.00s)
...
SUCCESS! -- 218 Passed | 0 Failed | 0 Pending | 0 Skipped

results.notes: Failed due to go.sum changes from code generation - expected in clean container. Command works but requires generated files to be committed.

validation.summary: Deps install OK, build OK, lint OK (0 issues), tests OK (218 passed). Code generation check requires committed files.

gaps: Integration tests exist but require -tags integration flag and were not validated (may need external services)
verify-generate check requires generated docs to be committed and in sync
FIPS compliance check requires check-payload tool and was not validated
Multi-architecture builds not validated (only amd64 tested)
kube-rbac-proxy subproject tests not separately validated
notes: Excellent test coverage with 218+ unit tests. Clear separation of unit and integration tests via build tags. All core validation commands (deps, build, lint, test) work perfectly in a standard Go container. FIPS builds require special tooling but standard builds are straightforward. Project uses modern Go practices (modules, workspaces, build tags).
